steps.title: Identify the required explanation type
steps.content: The student must “specify the reason the Pleiades' appearance changed”—this requires a causal explanation, not just a description of the change.

steps.title: Locate the causal reason
steps.content: C provides the causal mechanism: two stars moved so close together they appear as one, explaining why the cluster now shows six stars instead of seven. A mentions only cultural references, B shows the change without explaining it, and D contradicts itself by saying the cluster has seven stars while appearing to have six. That matches choice C — C is correct.
